title: Agent Control Contract — A2A method set & core wire types
description: The A2A (Agent2Agent) JSON-RPC method set and the core object types a conformant agent serves. This is the method-registry companion to the full type schema; it enumerates the served methods and points params/result at the shared $defs. Extracted from agent RFC 0020 (the reference binding) + the A2A specification (https://a2a-protocol.org); reference source crates/agentd/src/mcp/a2a.rs (dispatch) and capabilities.rs (a2a_surface). Per P0 the contract depends on this shape, never on a specific binary; agentd is the reference impl. The agent serves ONLY the live task core (SendMessage/GetTask/CancelTask/ListTasks + the streaming pair); the gateway owns durable history, version negotiation, OAuth/OIDC, webhooks, and the push-notification-config + getAuthenticatedExtendedCard methods (agentctl RFC 0013). CONTRACT 2.0 BINDING (the v1 P2 open question is now RESOLVED — see x-contract-2-binding): the agent serves A2A over mTLS HTTPS (POST /mcp, SSE for streaming), NOT the retired vsock/unix substrate, and the normative wire name is the A2A spec §9 BARE PascalCase form (SendMessage, GetTask, …). The per-method 'reference_method' below keeps the legacy a2a.-prefixed spelling (still ACCEPTED for back-compat) and 'spec_method' records the A2A slash-form for reference; the bare form is what agentd v2 EMITS and is canonical.

x-contract-2-binding.resolved: The v1 P2 binding-conformance question is settled in contract 2.0.
x-contract-2-binding.transport: mTLS HTTPS, JSON-RPC 2.0 over POST /mcp; streaming is an SSE text/event-stream of StreamResponse frames terminated by the terminal task state + stream close (no non-spec `final` flag).
x-contract-2-binding.normative_name: The A2A spec §9 BARE PascalCase method name (SendMessage, GetTask, CancelTask, ListTasks, SendStreamingMessage, SubscribeToTask) — i.e. the per-entry reference_method with the `a2a.` prefix stripped.
x-contract-2-binding.accepted_aliases: The legacy a2a.-prefixed spelling (reference_method) is still accepted for back-compat; the A2A slash-form (spec_method) is NOT served by agentd v2.
x-contract-2-binding.gating: A2A methods require a Management-origin (mTLS client-cert) caller; the agentctl gateway relays under the control-plane client cert.

protocol_version.description: A2A protocolVersion the agent advertises (surfaces.a2a.version, projected into the Agent Card). The reference impl pins '1.0' (capabilities.rs a2a_surface; mcp/a2a.rs module doc). PROVISIONAL: the gateway-served outward version may differ from the agent-advertised version (agentctl RFC 0013 §3.4); the gateway translates. Free string, not a const.

transport.description: Where the agent serves these methods. Contract 2.0: the A2A surface is served over the SAME mTLS HTTPS management listener (POST /mcp, SSE for streaming), gated to a PeerOrigin::Management (verified mTLS client-cert) peer (RFC 0015 §3.3); a Stdio peer gets -32601. The reference shares the management listener and omits surfaces.a2a.address (the v1 vsock/unix substrate was retired).

codec.const: JSON-RPC 2.0 over HTTPS POST /mcp; SSE text/event-stream for streaming

methods.description: The served method set. Contract 2.0: the normative wire name is the BARE PascalCase form (reference_method with the a2a. prefix stripped). Each entry records the legacy reference spelling (still accepted for back-compat), the A2A-spec slash-form (reference only, not served by v2), and $refs into $defs for params/result. served_by: 'live' = the agent serves it; 'gateway' = the agent returns -32601 and the gateway owns it. The agent serves ONLY the six 'live' methods (mcp/a2a.rs dispatch; capabilities.rs a2a_surface methods[]).

error_codes.description: The JSON-RPC error codes the A2A surface returns (RFC 0020 / A2A spec; mcp/a2a.rs). Closed set.

TASK_NOT_FOUND.description: GetTask/CancelTask/SubscribeToTask for an id not in the live registry (mcp/a2a.rs TASK_NOT_FOUND).

UNSUPPORTED_OPERATION.description: A2A spec §5.4 UnsupportedOperationError — e.g. CancelTask on an already-terminal task (mcp/a2a.rs UNSUPPORTED_OPERATION).

METHOD_NOT_FOUND.description: Unsupported a2a method, or a Stdio-origin peer, or a gateway-owned method the agent does not serve.

INVALID_PARAMS.description: e.g. SendMessage with no non-empty text part.

MethodEntry.description: One A2A method. Contract 2.0: the normative wire name is the BARE PascalCase form (reference_method minus the a2a. prefix). reference_method is the legacy a2a.-prefixed spelling (still accepted); spec_method is the A2A slash-form (reference only, not served by v2). params/result $ref the shared types.

reference_method.description: The legacy a2a.<PascalCase> dotted spelling — still ACCEPTED by the reference for back-compat. Contract 2.0: the agent EMITS and the gateway SENDS the BARE PascalCase form (this value with the a2a. prefix stripped, e.g. SendMessage); the bare name is canonical.

spec_method.description: A2A-spec slash-form JSON-RPC method (since 0.2.x), recorded for reference. Contract 2.0: agentd does NOT serve the slash-form — the normative binding is the bare PascalCase name.

served_by.description: live = the agent serves it (the live task core); gateway = the agent returns -32601, the gateway owns it (HTTP/SSE/push/durable history).

params.description: $ref into $defs for the request params type (omitted for no-param/gateway-owned methods).

result.description: $ref into $defs for the result type (omitted for gateway-owned methods).

streaming.description: true ⇒ the result is an SSE StreamResponse frame stream terminated by the terminal task STATE + stream close, NOT a unary reply (see StreamResponse). Contract 2.0 emits no non-spec `final` flag.

x-entries.note: configuration.returnImmediately defaults true ⇒ async WORKING Task to poll; false ⇒ block to terminal Task (mcp/a2a.rs send_message).
Unknown id ⇒ -32001. Completed run carries the distillate artifact (distillate-only invariant, RFC 0009 §8).
Live run ⇒ CANCELED; already-terminal ⇒ its real terminal state (cancel-of-finished is a read). Unknown id ⇒ -32001.
LIVE instance-local registry only; durable cross-pod history is gateway-held (RFC 0020 §5/§7). Pagination params accepted; nextPageToken omitted (registry bounded by MAX_SESSIONS).

Status-level streaming over SSE: writes statusUpdate{WORKING}, then (on completion) artifactUpdate{distillate,lastChunk:true}, then RETURNS statusUpdate{<terminal>} and closes the stream. Same-id frames; read until a terminal task state (no `final` flag in v2).
Resubscribe to an existing run by id. Unknown ⇒ -32001 (nothing streamed). Already-terminal ⇒ artifact (completed-only) + terminal status frame then close; live ⇒ WORKING then poll to terminal.
Gateway-owned; agent ⇒ -32601. The base card is the capabilities manifest (RFC 0015 §5.2).

TaskState.description: A2A TaskState. Contract 2.0: the reference wire carries the proto-enum spelling in status.state (mcp/a2a.rs TaskState::as_str — TASK_STATE_COMPLETED, TASK_STATE_WORKING, …); reference_state records that normative form and a2a_state records the lowercase A2A JSON-RPC binding for reference. The four terminal states are completed/failed/canceled/rejected; submitted/working are in-flight; input-required/auth-required/unknown(unspecified) are non-terminal interaction/unknown states the reference agent never produces but a richer gateway peer might.

reference_state.description: FROZEN proto-enum strings (a2a.proto), the wire form the reference emits.

x-terminal-status-mapping.description: agent TerminalStatus (RFC 0007 §3.4) → A2A TaskState (mcp/a2a.rs terminal_to_state). Closed mapping. Synthetic 'running' → working; unknown → failed (never silently working).

Role.description: Message role. Contract 2.0: the reference emits/consumes the proto spelling (ROLE_USER, send_message_params) as normative; the lowercase A2A JSON-RPC binding (user/agent) is recorded for reference.

Part.description: An A2A message/artifact Part. The reference produces/consumes only the {text} variant (instruction_from_message reads only text parts; artifacts carry one text Part); other variants are tolerated (additive). Open object.

Message.description: An A2A Message. parts is required (≥1). The reference concatenates the text parts in order into the run instruction (mcp/a2a.rs instruction_from_message); a SendMessage with no non-empty text part ⇒ -32602. contextId/taskId support multi-turn/steering (P-attach-gate: a no-puppeting tenant may have this omitted at the manifest level).

Artifact.description: An A2A Artifact. parts is required (≥1). On a COMPLETED task the reference attaches EXACTLY ONE artifact — the distillate — with artifactId '<taskId>.distillate' and one text Part (mcp/a2a.rs task_object / artifact_update_frame; the distillate-only invariant, RFC 0009 §8). No partial-artifact streaming.

TaskStatus.description: An A2A TaskStatus: { state, timestamp? }. state carries a TaskState wire string (reference: a TASK_STATE_* proto string). timestamp is RFC 3339 millis, minted now (the agent is stateless; durable per-transition history is gateway-held).

state.description: A TaskState wire string — reference TASK_STATE_* (TaskState.reference_state) or, post-binding, an a2a_state lowercase string.

Task.description: An A2A Task (RFC 0020 §5). A served run IS a Task; id is the served handle (e.g. served.N). artifacts present ONLY on a terminal COMPLETED task (distillate-only invariant) — absent on working/failed/canceled/rejected. contextId is derived deterministically from the handle ('ctx-<handle>'). history is gateway-held (the agent is stateless).

artifacts.description: Present only on COMPLETED; the reference emits exactly one (the distillate).

TaskStatusUpdateEvent.description: The statusUpdate arm of a StreamResponse (A2A TaskStatusUpdateEvent). Contract 2.0: the agent emits a WORKING opener and a terminal-state closer, then CLOSES the SSE stream — termination is by the terminal task STATE + stream close, so agentd emits no `final` flag. `final` remains OPTIONAL in the type (a richer gateway peer MAY set it), but a consumer MUST key termination off status.state.is_terminal() + stream close, NOT off `final`.

final.description: OPTIONAL legacy stream-termination flag. Contract 2.0: agentd does NOT emit it; termination is the terminal task state + SSE stream close. Consumers MUST read same-id frames as a stream until a terminal task state, NOT rely on `final`.

TaskArtifactUpdateEvent.description: The artifactUpdate arm of a StreamResponse (A2A TaskArtifactUpdateEvent). Carries the distillate as ONE Artifact with one text Part; lastChunk:true marks the whole artifact (the agent never chunks — distillate-only, RFC 0009 §8). Emitted ONLY for a completed run (mcp/a2a.rs artifact_update_frame).

StreamResponse.description: The A2A StreamResponse oneof: { task | message | statusUpdate | artifactUpdate }. Carried as the 'result' of a same-id JSON-RPC reply frame: for one streaming request id the agent emits SEVERAL frames {jsonrpc,id,result:<StreamResponse>} over an SSE text/event-stream (mcp/a2a.rs module doc — the agent↔gateway streaming convention). The agent emits ONLY the statusUpdate and artifactUpdate variants (status-level streaming). CONSUMER CONTRACT (contract 2.0): read same-id frames as a STREAM until a terminal task state + stream close (agentd emits no `final` flag); do NOT treat them as duplicate unary replies. P-seq (OPEN): no monotonic per-frame seq, so gap-free SSE resume (Last-Event-ID) is not guaranteed — the gateway falls back to terminal-state reconstruction (agentctl RFC 0013 §3.3).

SendMessageParams.description: Params for SendMessage / SendStreamingMessage (A2A SendMessageRequest). configuration.returnImmediately defaults true (async WORKING Task to poll); false blocks to a terminal Task (mcp/a2a.rs send_message). For SendStreamingMessage the run is executed synchronously while the lifecycle is streamed.

GetTaskParams.description: Params for GetTask: { id, historyLength? } (mcp/a2a.rs task_id_param reads id). Unknown id ⇒ -32001.

TaskIdParams.description: Params for CancelTask / SubscribeToTask: { id } (mcp/a2a.rs task_id_param). Unknown id ⇒ -32001.

ListTasksParams.description: Params for ListTasks: { pageSize?, pageToken?, filter? }. Accepted but the ephemeral live registry returns the whole bounded set; nextPageToken omitted (mcp/a2a.rs list_tasks).

ListTasksResult.description: Result for ListTasks: { tasks }. LIVE instance-local registry only (mcp/a2a.rs list_tasks). nextPageToken omitted by the reference.
